Section 2 — Visuals & Data

Chart 1 — Supplier Count by Province (Top 15) — All 4,654 Companies

All 4,654 BOI companies (Tiers 1–4). Bar label = count (% of total). Red = Critical Eastern Seaboard · Amber = High concentration (≥200) · Blue = Moderate. Top 2 provinces (Chon Buri + Rayong) account for 42.4% of all companies.

Chart 2 — Ownership Classification

All 4,654 BOI companies (Tiers 1–4) reclassified into 6 ownership groups. Chinese-Sphere = Chinese + Taiwanese + Singaporean + HK + Korean. JV/Mixed includes joint ventures. Counts & percentages on donut and bar.

Chart 3 — Industry Composition × Province — All 4,654 Companies

Top 15 provinces by total BOI company count, sorted highest at top. Stacked by industry group (17 categories); label shown when ≥35 companies. Right-end label = province total. Based on all 4,654 companies (Tiers 1–4).

Chart 4 — Province Concentration × Industry — Top 12 Industries

Top 12 industries by total company count. Each segment shows the province's contribution; label shown when ≥30 companies. Top 10 provinces highlighted, remainder grouped as “Other Provinces”. Shows geographic clustering of each industry across Thailand. Based on all 4,654 companies.

Chart 4b — Category Composition × Industry

Top 5 categories within each of the top 12 industries. Segment label = count (% of industry total). Reveals the dominant product/service categories that define each industry cluster. All 4,654 companies.

Chart 5 — Thailand Geographic Concentration Map 🔍 Interactive — Zoom & Click

Bubble size proportional to total company count per province (all 4,654 BOI companies). Click any bubble for industry & ownership breakdown. Scroll / pinch to zoom · Drag to pan · Red = Critical Eastern Seaboard · Amber = High (≥200) · Blue = Significant (50–199) · Green = Moderate.

Chart 6 — Ownership Nationality × Province — All 4,654 Companies

Top 15 provinces by total BOI company count, stacked by nationality group. Thai, Chinese, JV/Mixed, Japanese, Taiwanese, Singaporean, Korean, Hong Kong, Western, Other. Label shown when ≥30 companies per segment. Based on all 4,654 companies (Tiers 1–4).

Section 5 — Executive Insights

Geographic Concentration Risk

  • Critical Chon Buri is the #1 province: 216 CE suppliers (37.1%). CE density 38.6% — highest of any Thai province.
  • Critical Rayong: 113 identified + 116 UNESTABLISHED IC/Chip = maximum 229. Eastern Seaboard dual-province dependency: 56.5% of all CE suppliers.
  • Watch Top 5 provinces hold 79.2% of CE suppliers in a tight Eastern corridor (Chon Buri, Rayong, Samut Prakan, Phra Nakhon Si Ayutthaya, Chachoengsao).
  • Positive Pathum Thani (25) and Prachin Buri (24) provide Central Thailand diversification. CE density 25.8% and 32.4% respectively.
  • Opportunity Lamphun (6, 46.2% density) and Chiang Mai (4, 14.8%) in the North show nascent electronics clusters — long-term diversification targets.

Commodity & Capability Concentration

  • Watch PCBA / PCB: 54 PCBA-SMT + 14 PCB Multilayer = 68 companies (11.7%) — key electronics manufacturing cluster.
  • Note Electrical Appliance Parts (41, 7.0%) and Cable & Wire Harness (39, 6.7%) are 2nd and 3rd largest categories.
  • Note Optical / Telecom Components (32, 5.5%) + Semiconductors (19, 3.3%) complete a high-tech supply chain cluster.
  • Gap 116 UNESTABLISHED IC/Chip in Rayong = unquantified additional exposure. Field survey required.
  • Positive Home Appliances: 506 fully relevant suppliers (86.9%) — deepest CE product ecosystem.

Ownership Structure Risk

  • Critical 70.8% Chinese-Sphere (Mainland 283, Taiwanese 60, Singaporean 36, HK 23, Korean 10) — structural geopolitical concentration risk.
  • Watch JV / Mixed (8.4%, 49) — mixed-nationality ownership, requires structure due diligence.
  • Note Japanese (9.1%, 53) — stable, typically lower geopolitical risk; good diversification anchors.
  • Watch Thai-Owned only 7.9% (46) — very low domestic ownership of CE supply base.
  • Action Develop domestic ownership programme targeting PCBA and cable/wire sub-categories.

Risk Profile & CE Product Coverage

  • Watch Medium Risk = 42.8% (249 companies) — largest risk tier.
  • Positive Low Risk: 40.9% (238 companies) — solid engagement-ready pipeline.
  • Note Low-Medium Risk: 16.3% (95 companies) — monitor and qualify.
  • Gap 116 UNESTABLISHED: zero risk rating — largest intelligence gap in the dataset.
  • Thin AR Glasses & Wearables: 257–263 full suppliers — significantly thinner ecosystem vs. legacy CE lines.

Section 7 — Data Notes & Limitations

Accepted Limitations

  • Note Province-level geocoding only — no facility addresses available. Supplier pins on map use province centroids ± jitter.
  • Note Nationality = BOI-registered corporate HQ origin, not operational nationality.
  • Note Taiwanese suppliers grouped in “Chinese-Sphere” for geographic proximity analysis only — geopolitical risk differs from Mainland PRC.
  • Gap 116 UNESTABLISHED IC/Chip placeholders in Rayong have no company names or risk ratings — field survey required.
  • Note Category-to-capability grouping is algorithmic. Validate with supply chain SME before sourcing decisions.
  • Note Data vintage: BOI registrations 2024–2025. Field verification recommended before engagement.
